name: Patient Records, HIPAA & Controlled Substances (US)
description: Reference policy aligned with HIPAA Privacy Rule, state medical board record-keeping rules, and DEA controlled-substances framework.

data.text: Clinical notes are clear, accurate, and detailed enough for another licensed clinician to continue care.
Records are retained per state law and at minimum for the HIPAA Privacy Rule period (typically 6 years).
Informed consent is documented for procedures, off-label prescribing, and treatments with material risks.
Advance directives, surrogate decision-makers, and POLST/MOLST forms are tracked and accessible to the care team.
E&M documentation supports billed level of service per current CMS / payer guidelines.
Controlled substances are prescribed and dispensed per DEA rules; PDMP queries are documented where required.
PHI disclosures, NPP acknowledgements, and BAAs with vendors are tracked and retained.

clauses.title: Notes sufficient for handover
clauses.body: Clinical notes must be clear, accurate, and detailed enough for another licensed clinician to continue care.

clauses.title: HIPAA / state retention
clauses.body: Records must be retained per state law and at minimum for the HIPAA Privacy Rule period (typically 6 years).

clauses.title: Informed consent documented
clauses.body: Informed consent must be documented for procedures, off-label prescribing, and treatments with material risks.

clauses.title: Advance directives tracked
clauses.body: Advance directives, surrogate decision-makers, and POLST/MOLST forms must be tracked and accessible.

clauses.title: E&M documentation supports billing
clauses.body: E&M documentation must support the billed level of service per CMS / payer guidelines.

clauses.title: DEA / PDMP compliance
clauses.body: Controlled substances must be prescribed and dispensed per DEA rules; PDMP queries documented where required.

clauses.title: HIPAA disclosures tracked
clauses.body: PHI disclosures, NPP acknowledgements, and BAAs must be tracked and retained.
